text:
The Foreign Office will assist the US in obtaining the concurrence of these interested countries. US Embassy London believes this is the most that can be obtained from the Foreign Office at this moment.

Comment: Great Britain, like France, had previously declined to agree to tripartite suspension of the overflight right, expressing the general belief that that particular weapon should be saved for possible future use in case of more vital importance to the West.

The French have reacted to the latest American overtures by suggesting that there should be agreement on a general program including conditions for re-establishment of the flights. The Belgians have indicated that they will not oppose suspension, the Dutch interests in the flights are not so great as to presage isolated opposition, and the Swiss attitude is unknown
